<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is blocked. The cells to my right are open floor, so I should turn right to face a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cells to my left side appear open, while the right side is blocked by a wall. I should turn left to face a safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. To my right there is open floor, while the left side is blocked by the wall column. I should turn right to face the safe open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. Both left and right adjacent directions appear blocked by walls in the nearby corridor, and the open safe path is behind me through the floor tiles above my current position. To head toward that safe route, I should turn around; the first step is to turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so moving forward is blocked. To my right there is open floor, while lava is farther ahead in that direction but not immediately adjacent. The safest next step is to turn right to face the open path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at the bottom-center facing upward. The cell directly in front is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. Both left and right adjacent paths are blocked by lava on the left and a wall on the right, but turning is safe. Since forward is unsafe and right is blocked by a wall corridor, I will turn left to reorient and look for a safer path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>

</action>

<thought>

</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is blocked. The open floor cells are to my right, so I should turn right to face a safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing upward, so the cell directly in front is the one above ^. That cell is lava (L), so moving forward would be deadly. Both left and right adjacent cells on the current row are safe floor cells, but turning right would face a wall immediately in front on the next move, while turning left would face an open floor cell. So the safest useful next action is to turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<action>
